docs.description: This tutorial walks you through setting up the complete Playrunner local development environment from scratch. By the end you'll have the local stack running, the workspace configured through the setup UI, and the product app ready for login.

docs.title: Understanding Test Reports
docs.description: Every Playrunner test run generates a full Playwright HTML report stored with a unique run ID. This tutorial shows you how to navigate the report, find failing tests, and access screenshots and traces.

metadata.title: Introducing Playrunner: Visual orchestration for Playwright teams
metadata.description: Playrunner brings Playwright workflows, runners, integrations, live feedback, and reports together so teams can ship with confidence.

frontMatter.title: Introducing Playrunner: Visual orchestration for Playwright teams
frontMatter.description: Playrunner brings Playwright workflows, runners, integrations, live feedback, and reports together so teams can ship with confidence.

blogPosts.content:
Today, we're releasing Playrunner, an early source-available visual
orchestration platform for teams running Playwright test automation.

Playwright is excellent at executing browser tests. But as a test suite grows
across a team, the work around those tests grows too: CI scripts, runner
infrastructure, environment configuration, reports, alerts, retries, and issue
workflows. Playrunner brings that orchestration layer into one place.

{/* truncate */}

## What Playrunner is for

Playrunner lets teams connect triggers, integrations, environment
configuration, and Playwright runners on a visual workflow canvas. A workflow
can run locally in isolated Docker containers or use the GCP execution path for
Cloud Run jobs.

As a run progresses, Playrunner streams node state and logs back to the editor.
When it finishes, the team can inspect the Playwright report and any captured
artifacts, including screenshots, videos, and traces. Integrations such as
GitHub, Jira, Slack, schedules, and environment nodes let test execution fit
into the delivery workflow around it.

## Helping teams ship with confidence

Playrunner is designed to reduce the glue teams maintain around end-to-end
testing.

- **Make delivery workflows repeatable.** Capture the steps and dependencies
  around Playwright tests once, then run the same workflow consistently.
- **Get to failures faster.** Follow node status and live logs while a workflow
  runs, then open the Playwright report for the evidence behind a result.
- **Give the whole team visibility.** The workflow canvas makes the delivery
  path easier to understand than orchestration spread across scripts and
  service configuration.
- **Connect testing to the rest of delivery.** Package-based integrations for
  repositories, schedules, environments, notifications, and work tracking let
  test results move into the tools a team already uses.
- **Start locally and scale when needed.** Isolated local runners and a GCP
  execution path give teams room to grow without changing the workflow model.

The goal is straightforward: spend less time stitching testing infrastructure
together, get useful feedback sooner, and make confident release decisions with
clearer evidence.

## Playrunner needs contributors

Playrunner is still early, and that is an invitation. The project needs
contributors across frontend and UX, runners, scheduling and retries,
integrations, reporting, documentation, infrastructure, and practical
AI-assisted testing workflows.

Because the project is still taking shape, contributors can influence the
architecture, product experience, integrations, and roadmap rather than only
working around settled decisions.

Start with the [contributing guide](https://playrunner.dev/docs/contributing),
explore the [roadmap](https://playrunner.dev/docs/roadmap), or pick a
[good first issue](https://github.com/playrunner/playrunner/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20is%3Aopen%20label%3A%22good%20first%20issue%22).

You can also [read the docs](https://playrunner.dev/docs/overview),
[run Playrunner locally](https://playrunner.dev/docs/tutorials/getting-started),
join the conversation in
[GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/playrunner/playrunner/discussions), or
meet the community on [Discord](https://discord.gg/23yz25kat).

Playrunner is available now at
[github.com/playrunner/playrunner](https://github.com/playrunner/playrunner).
